title: 3. Shading and wrinkles
text:
Shading from light is another factor that affects the
wrinkles  visible  in  a  photo.  If  the  patient’s  head  is
slightly facing sideways, up, or down, different wrinkles
can be more vivid or less visible. Therefore, the patient's
nose should be perfectly aligned to the middle of the
frame.  Activate  the  grid  when  you  take  a  shot.  The
camera should not be tilted to locate the nose on the
middle of the frame. It should be always perpendicular
with the patient’s face. Do not tilt, adjust the height of
the camera on the tripod, or use a height adjustable bar
stool for patients. Ring ﬂash and indirect lighting with a
soft box can help minimize shading.
